attributes.description: Alerts when the command rate drops below 1/sec. The cache is idle or unreachable by clients.
attributes.name: [Memcached OTel] Low command throughput

investigation_guide.blob:
## Memcached low command throughput

### What fired
Command rate dropped below 1/sec during the window.

### Why it matters
A near-zero command rate either means clients have all disconnected or stopped issuing requests. Application layer almost certainly has an outage.

### Triage
1. Check Memcached connection count.
2. Inspect client-side application health.
3. Verify network between clients and Memcached.

### Remediation
- Fix the client or network issue.

### Tuning
- Fires on < 1 cmd/sec. Filter out maintenance windows.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-memcachedreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE memcached.commands IS NOT NULL
// Total command rate across all types (get, set, flush, touch)
| STATS cmd_rate = SUM(RATE(memcached.commands))
  BY host.name, COALESCE(service.instance.id, host.name)
// Alert when throughput drops below 1 cmd/sec — indicates cache is idle or unreachable
| WHERE cmd_rate < 1.0
| SORT cmd_rate ASC
| LIMIT 20
